mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a Kubernetes distribution that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in enterprise environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to leverage Kubernetes with integrated tools and services for enhanced operational efficiency.

steps.title: Install Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the Tanzu CLI for version 2.5. Use the following commands to install on Linux:
```bash
wget https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -C /usr/local/bin -xzf tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tanzu version
```

steps.title: Bootstrap Management Cluster
steps.description:
Create a management cluster using the Tanzu CLI. Replace `<your-cluster-name>` and `<your-region>` with your desired values:
```bash
tanzu bootstrap management-cluster create --name <your-cluster-name> --plan dev --region <your-region>
```

steps.title: Create Workload Cluster
steps.description:
Create a workload cluster using the management cluster. Replace `<your-cluster-name>` and `<your-region>`:
```bash
tanzu cluster create <your-cluster-name> --plan dev --region <your-region>
```

steps.title: Connect kubectl to the Workload Cluster
steps.description:
Set the context for kubectl to connect to the newly created workload cluster:
```bash
kubectl config use-context <your-cluster-name>
```

steps.title: Verify Cluster Installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the cluster and nodes:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get nodes
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Enable Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for the workload cluster:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/vertical-pod-autoscaler/deploy/vpa-operator.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Delete Workload Cluster
uninstall.description:
To remove the workload cluster, use the following command:
```bash
tanzu cluster delete <your-cluster-name>
```

uninstall.title: Delete Management Cluster
uninstall.description:
To remove the management cluster, use:
```bash
tanzu bootstrap management-cluster delete --name <your-cluster-name>
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
upgrade.description:
To upgrade from version 2.4 to 2.5, first backup your cluster configuration and data. Use the following command to upgrade:
```bash
tanzu cluster upgrade <your-cluster-name> --version 2.5
```
Ensure you check the upgrade notes for any breaking changes or migration steps.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster Not Responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the cluster is not responding, check the status of the nodes:
```bash
kubectl get nodes
```
If nodes are NotReady, check the logs for kubelet and other components for errors.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl Context Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot connect to the cluster, ensure the correct context is set:
```bash
kubectl config get-contexts
```
Switch context if necessary using `kubectl config use-context <context-name>`.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas Exceeded
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource quota issues, check the resource usage:
```bash
kubectl describe quota -n <namespace>
```
Adjust your resource requests or limits accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Enhanced support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI commands for cluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy commands in favor of new CLI structure.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new features for workload management and improved integration with Tanzu Mission Control.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older versions of Kubernetes support.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid will have a management cluster and at least one workload cluster running, with kubectl configured to interact with the workload cluster. All nodes should be in a Ready state.
codeSnippets:
wget https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -C /usr/local/bin -xzf tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tanzu version

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Tanzu CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
